purpose: Declare ownership boundaries for model families where current architecture is reasonable but overlap risk remains.

ownership_specs.boundary_rule: Project construction contracts own project commercial commitments; professional income/expense models specialize direction; general contracts own procurement/general commitments outside the project contract center.
ownership_specs.forbidden_drift: Do not let sc.general.contract, construction.contract, and construction.contract.income/expense independently own the same commitment without a declared direction and source record.
ownership_specs.decision: Keep all four surfaces for now, but require new contract fields to declare whether they describe income commitment, expense commitment, general procurement, or derived reconciliation.

ownership_specs.boundary_rule: Fund account is account master data; treasury reconciliation is an auditable reconciliation document; treasury ledger and fund summaries are rebuildable read models.
ownership_specs.forbidden_drift: Do not write cash movement workflow facts directly into ledger/summary models, and do not use fund account master as a transaction table.
ownership_specs.decision: Keep account master, reconciliation, and ledger separate; add new treasury behavior only to the layer that owns the fact.

ownership_specs.boundary_rule: Native product owns product identity and integration; material catalog owns construction-visible classification, legacy material surface, and industry search semantics.
ownership_specs.forbidden_drift: Do not create a second independent product identity in sc.material.catalog; catalog records must map or project to native product identity when operational flow requires it.
ownership_specs.decision: Treat product.template as identity root and material catalog as construction-industry surface.

ownership_specs.boundary_rule: Payment request owns business intent and approval; payment execution owns confirmed payment fact; expense claim owns reimbursement/deposit/deduction claims before settlement or execution.
ownership_specs.forbidden_drift: Do not mark a payment request as the final cash fact unless sc.payment.execution or an accounting integration fact exists.
ownership_specs.decision: Keep request, claim, and execution separate, with projections aggregating their state instead of replacing them.

ownership_specs.boundary_rule: Projection models are deterministic management views sourced from native, industry, formal, or legacy facts; refresh code owns their content.
ownership_specs.forbidden_drift: Do not add primary workflow writes to projection models, and do not make a projection the only place where a business fact exists.
ownership_specs.decision: Projection models stay read/refresh owned; future iteration should register refresh dependencies rather than append more one-off scripts.
